questions.question: During the boot-up sequence of a modern computer, which firmware is primarily responsible for initializing hardware components and loading the operating system's bootloader from a storage device?

options.b: BIOS (Basic Input/Output System) or UEFI (Unified Extensible Firmware Interface)

questions.question: In MS-Excel, you have a formula in cell C2 which is '=A2*B$1'. If you copy this formula and paste it into cell C3, what will the new formula in C3 be?

You need to create personalized invitation letters for 100 different guests using a single template in MS-Word. The guest details (Name, Address) are stored in an MS-Excel sheet. Which feature in MS-Word is most efficient for this task?
In HTML, which of the following tags is an example of an inline element, meaning it does not start on a new line and only takes up as much width as necessary?

To ensure a consistent look and feel (e.g., company logo, font style, and color scheme) across all slides in a large PowerPoint presentation, which feature should you use to apply these changes globally?

options.a: Applying a Theme to each slide individually
options.c: Copying and pasting elements onto each slide
options.d: Inserting a Header and Footer on every slide

questions.question: The universal gravitational constant (G) has a value of 6.67 x 10⁻¹¹ N m²/kg² in the SI system. What is its value in the CGS system (dyne cm²/g²)?

A physical quantity P is related to four observables a, b, c, and d as P = (a³b²) / (√c * d). The percentage errors in the measurement of a, b, c, and d are 1%, 2%, 4%, and 2% respectively. What is the percentage error in the quantity P?

While measuring the diameter of a sphere with a Vernier caliper (Least Count = 0.01 cm), the Main Scale Reading (MSR) is 3.2 cm. The 6th division of the Vernier scale coincides with a division on the main scale. If the instrument has a positive zero error of +0.04 cm, what is the correct diameter of the sphere?

A student measures the thickness of a wire using a micrometer screw gauge with a least count of 0.01 mm. The main scale reading is 5.5 mm and the 28th division of the circular scale coincides with the reference line. The screw gauge has a negative zero error of -0.05 mm. The correct thickness of the wire is:
Which of the following pairs of physical quantities does NOT have the same dimensional formula?
The length of a rod is measured in an experiment and the readings are found to be 5.21 cm, 5.24 cm, 5.25 cm, 5.20 cm, and 5.25 cm. The percentage error in the measurement is approximately:
A body of mass 10 kg, initially at rest, is acted upon by a constant force. If it acquires a velocity of 20 m/s after 5 seconds, what is the magnitude of the force?

A cricket ball of mass 150 g moving with a speed of 30 m/s is hit by a bat and returns along the same path with a speed of 20 m/s. If the ball is in contact with the bat for 0.01 seconds, what is the average force exerted by the bat? (Note the change in direction of velocity)
A particle moves in a circle of radius 20 cm. If its linear speed is 10 m/s, what is its angular velocity?
An object moving in a circle at a constant speed has an acceleration that is:

options.a: zero because the speed is constant.
options.b: directed tangent to the circular path.
options.c: directed away from the center of the circle.
options.d: directed towards the center of the circle.

questions.question: A pump is required to lift 600 kg of water per minute from a well 25 m deep and eject it with a speed of 50 m/s. What is the power required to perform this task? (Assume g = 10 m/s²)
The potential energy of a system increases if work is done:

options.a: by the system against a conservative force.
options.b: by a conservative force on the system.
options.c: by the system against a non-conservative force.
options.d: upon the system by a non-conservative force.

questions.question: A wire of resistance 4 Ω is stretched to twice its original length. Assuming the volume of the wire remains constant and the resistivity of the material does not change, what will be its new resistance?

Two point charges are placed a certain distance apart in air and exert a force F on each other. The charges are then placed the same distance apart in a medium with a dielectric constant K=5. What is the new force between the charges?

A potential difference of 20 V is applied across a conductor. If 2.5 x 10¹⁹ electrons pass through a cross-section of the conductor in 2 seconds, what is its resistance? (Charge of an electron, e = 1.6 x 10⁻¹⁹ C)
How should three resistors of 2 Ω, 3 Ω, and 6 Ω be connected to produce an equivalent resistance of 4 Ω?

options.c: 2 Ω resistor in series with the parallel combination of 3 Ω and 6 Ω resistors
options.d: 3 Ω resistor in series with the parallel combination of 2 Ω and 6 Ω resistors

questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor with air as the dielectric has a capacitance of 9 pF. The distance between the plates is now reduced to half, and the space between them is completely filled with a dielectric slab of dielectric constant K = 6. What is the new capacitance?
Calculate the work done in moving a charge of 4 µC from a point A with an electric potential of 10 V to a point B with an electric potential of -5 V.
At what temperature is the value on the Fahrenheit scale exactly double the value on the Celsius scale?
A thermos flask (vacuum flask) is designed to minimize heat transfer. The vacuum created between its double walls is primarily effective in preventing heat transfer by which mode(s)?

Two solid spheres, one of aluminium and the other of lead, have the same mass. The specific heat capacity of aluminium is significantly higher than that of lead. If both spheres are heated to the same initial temperature (e.g., 100°C) and then placed on a thick block of ice, which statement is true?

options.a: The aluminium sphere will melt more ice.
options.b: The lead sphere will melt more ice.
options.c: Both spheres will melt the same amount of ice.
options.d: The amount of ice melted depends on their density, not specific heat.

questions.question: A steel railway track has a length of 20 meters at a temperature of 10°C. If the temperature rises to 40°C during the day, what will be the increase in its length? (Given, coefficient of linear expansion for steel, α = 1.2 x 10⁻⁵ /°C).
The SI unit for the coefficient of thermal conductivity (k) is:
A faulty thermometer has its fixed points marked as 5° and 95°. When this thermometer reads 68°, what is the correct temperature in Celsius?
A progressive wave travels from a rarer medium to a denser medium. Which of the following properties of the wave remains constant?
According to Sabine's formula for reverberation time (T), if the volume (V) of a concert hall is doubled and the total absorption (A) is halved, the new reverberation time (T') will be:

In an optical fiber, the light is guided through the core due to the phenomenon of total internal reflection. This is only possible if the refractive index of the core (n1) and the refractive index of the cladding (n2) satisfy which condition?
The displacement of a particle in a medium due to a wave is represented by the equation y(x,t) = 0.05 sin(50πt - πx), where all quantities are in SI units. What is the periodic time of the wave?

LASER is an acronym for Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation. The primary characteristic that distinguishes LASER light from the light produced by an ordinary incandescent bulb is its:
The critical angle for a light ray travelling from a transparent medium into air is 30°. What is the absolute refractive index of the medium? (Given sin 30° = 0.5)

When the chemical equation for the reaction between aqueous lead(II) nitrate and aqueous potassium iodide, which forms a precipitate of lead(II) iodide and aqueous potassium nitrate, is balanced using the smallest whole-number integers, what are the stoichiometric coefficients for potassium iodide (KI) and potassium nitrate (KNO₃) respectively?

A student observes that when solid silver chloride is exposed to sunlight, it slowly darkens and turns into greyish silver metal, releasing a pungent gas. The reaction is represented as: 2AgCl(s) → 2Ag(s) + Cl₂(g). This transformation can be classified as which of the following types of reactions?

An iron nail is placed in a test tube containing a bright blue solution of copper(II) sulphate. After about 30 minutes, it is observed that the blue colour of the solution has faded and a reddish-brown coating has formed on the surface of the nail. Which statement provides the most accurate chemical explanation for these observations?

options.a: It is a double displacement reaction where iron and copper ions exchange places, forming a colourless compound.
options.b: It is a combination reaction where iron combines with the sulphate to form a new reddish-brown complex, consuming the blue copper ions.
options.c: It is a displacement reaction where more reactive iron displaces copper from the solution to form copper metal (reddish-brown coating) and pale green iron(II) sulphate, causing the blue colour to fade.
options.d: It is a decomposition reaction where copper(II) sulphate breaks down in the presence of iron, depositing copper and making the solution colourless.

questions.question: A student is given four substances: sulphur powder, a piece of amorphous carbon (coal), a graphite rod, and an iodine crystal. Which of these substances, despite being a non-metal or an allotrope of a non-metal, would allow a simple electrical circuit to be completed and a bulb to glow brightly when used as an electrode?

In a laboratory setup, four different metal samples are subjected to different conditions. Metal A is placed in cold water. Metal B is placed in hot water. Steam is passed over Metal C. Metal D is also placed in cold water. Vigorous effervescence is seen with Metal A. Metal B starts floating after some time due to bubbles of gas sticking to its surface. Metal C forms an oxide that is reddish-brown. Metal D shows no reaction. Identify the correct sequence of metals A, B, C, and D.

options.a: A: Sodium, B: Magnesium, C: Iron, D: Copper
options.b: A: Magnesium, B: Calcium, C: Zinc, D: Iron
options.c: A: Iron, B: Sodium, C: Copper, D: Magnesium
options.d: A: Sodium, B: Iron, C: Magnesium, D: Zinc

questions.question: An ionic compound, say sodium chloride (NaCl), is formed by the transfer of electrons. Consider the following statements regarding its properties and the related concept of metal corrosion. Which statement is entirely correct?

options.a: Solid NaCl does not conduct electricity, but a solution of NaCl in kerosene will, because the ions become mobile.
options.b: Ionic compounds have high melting points due to strong inter-ionic attraction; this is why rust (an ionic oxide of iron) is a hard solid that flakes off easily.
options.c: The presence of dissolved ionic compounds like NaCl in water accelerates the corrosion of iron by increasing the electrical conductivity of the water, which acts as an electrolyte in the electrochemical cell formed on the iron's surface.
options.d: During the formation of NaCl, the sodium atom gains a positive charge by gaining a proton, making it a cation.

questions.question: When a small amount of black Copper(II) oxide is added to a test tube containing dilute Hydrochloric acid, a reaction occurs. Identify the major products formed and the subsequent colour change observed in the solution.

options.a: Copper(II) chloride and water; the solution turns blue-green.
options.b: Copper(I) chloride and hydrogen gas; the solution turns colourless.
options.c: Copper(II) sulphate and water; the solution turns deep blue.
options.d: Copper(II) hydroxide and chlorine gas; a precipitate is formed.

questions.question: A student observes that dry hydrogen chloride (HCl) gas does not change the colour of a dry blue litmus paper, but it turns a moist blue litmus paper red. Which statement provides the most accurate scientific reason for this observation?

options.a: The moisture on the litmus paper acts as a catalyst for the acidic reaction.
options.b: Water is essential for the dissociation of HCl into H+ and Cl- ions, and these H+ ions are responsible for the acidic property.
options.c: Dry HCl gas is neutral, and it becomes an acid only upon contact with water.
options.d: The blue dye in the litmus paper is insoluble in HCl gas but dissolves in water to react.

questions.question: Tooth enamel, primarily composed of calcium phosphate (Ca3(PO4)2), is the hardest substance in the body. It starts to corrode when the pH in the mouth drops below a critical value. What is this approximate critical pH, and how does the use of a typical toothpaste help prevent this corrosion?

options.a: Critical pH is 5.5; toothpastes are generally basic and neutralize the excess acid.
options.b: Critical pH is 7.0; toothpastes are neutral and help in washing away the acid.
options.c: Critical pH is 4.5; toothpastes contain fluoride which creates a new protective layer.
options.d: Critical pH is 6.5; toothpastes are slightly acidic to kill the bacteria effectively.

questions.question: A salt's acidic or basic nature depends on the strength of the parent acid and base from which it is formed. Which one of the following salts, when dissolved in pure water, will result in a solution with a pH value greater than 7?
In a typical freshwater pond ecosystem, the decomposers, such as bacteria and fungi, play a crucial role. What is their primary function in the nutrient cycle?

options.a: To directly provide food for the primary consumers (herbivores).
options.b: To convert solar energy into chemical energy through photosynthesis.
options.c: To break down dead organic matter and release inorganic nutrients back into the ecosystem.
options.d: To consume producers and regulate their population.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal in Agra is famously threatened by a form of pollution that causes its white marble to yellow. This is primarily an effect of 'acid rain'. Which two air pollutants are the main precursors to acid rain?

The 'Greenhouse Effect' is a natural phenomenon essential for life on Earth, but its enhancement due to human activities is causing global warming. Which statement accurately describes the mechanism of the enhanced greenhouse effect?

options.a: Greenhouse gases in the atmosphere block incoming solar radiation, causing the Earth to cool down.
options.b: Greenhouse gases deplete the ozone layer, allowing more harmful ultraviolet radiation to reach the Earth's surface.
options.c: Greenhouse gases are transparent to incoming shortwave solar radiation but trap outgoing longwave infrared radiation (heat), warming the atmosphere.
options.d: Greenhouse gases react with atmospheric water to form acid rain, which increases the temperature of water bodies.

questions.question: Hydropower is a major source of renewable energy in India. However, the construction of large dams for this purpose often has significant environmental and social consequences. Which of the following is considered a major limitation or disadvantage of large-scale hydroelectric power projects?

options.a: They produce a large amount of greenhouse gas emissions during operation.
options.b: The cost of electricity produced is much higher than that from fossil fuels.
options.c: They are unreliable as they can only generate power during the daytime.
options.d: They can cause submergence of large areas of forest and agricultural land, and displace local communities.

questions.question: Geothermal energy harnesses heat from the Earth's interior. While it is a consistent and reliable source of energy, its widespread availability is limited. What is the primary requirement for a location to be suitable for setting up a geothermal power plant?

options.a: The location must have strong and consistent wind throughout the year.
options.b: The location must be at a high altitude in a mountainous region.
options.c: The location must be in a tectonically active area where magma is close to the Earth's surface, providing a high-temperature heat source.
options.d: The location must be on a coastline with a large difference between high and low tides.

questions.question:
Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow.

In today's rapidly evolving job market, digital literacy is no longer a luxury but a fundamental necessity. It encompasses a wide range of skills, from basic computer operation to understanding complex software, navigating online platforms, and discerning credible information from misinformation. For a young professional entering the workforce, proficiency in digital tools is as crucial as their core technical knowledge. Employers now expect candidates to be comfortable with collaborative software like Google Workspace or Microsoft 365, possess basic data analysis skills using spreadsheets, and maintain a professional online presence through platforms like LinkedIn.

However, true digital literacy goes beyond just technical know-how. It also involves a critical understanding of digital citizenship, which includes concepts of online security, data privacy, and ethical online conduct. An employee who carelessly clicks on a phishing email can compromise an entire company's network. Similarly, someone who doesn't understand the permanence of a digital footprint might post inappropriate content that could harm their career prospects indefinitely.

Therefore, educational institutions have a significant role to play. Merely teaching students how to use a specific application is not enough. The curriculum must integrate a holistic approach to digital literacy, teaching students how to adapt to new technologies, think critically about the information they consume online, and behave responsibly in the digital world. This proactive approach ensures that graduates are not just job-ready but also future-proof, equipped to handle the technological challenges and ethical dilemmas of tomorrow's workplace.

What is the primary message conveyed in the passage?

options.a: Schools should focus only on teaching the latest software applications.
options.b: LinkedIn is the most important tool for all young professionals.
options.c: Digital literacy is a comprehensive and essential skill for modern career success.
options.d: Core technical knowledge is more important than digital skills.

questions.question: According to the passage, digital citizenship involves understanding all of the following EXCEPT:

options.b: The principles of ethical online conduct.
options.c: The methods for creating complex software.

questions.question: What does the passage imply about a person's 'digital footprint'?

options.a: It is temporary and can be easily erased.
options.b: It only includes professional activities on platforms like LinkedIn.
options.c: It is irrelevant to an individual's career prospects.
options.d: It can have long-lasting effects on a person's professional life.

questions.question: In the final paragraph, the author uses the term 'holistic approach'. What does 'holistic' most nearly mean in this context?

options.a: Focusing only on the most difficult technical aspects.
options.b: Including all parts of a subject; comprehensive.
options.c: A method that is new and untested.

questions.question: The author suggests that the role of educational institutions in teaching digital literacy should be:

options.a: Limited to basic computer operation skills.
options.b: Reactive, by only teaching skills as they become popular.
options.c: Proactive and integrated, covering technical, critical, and ethical skills.
options.d: Optional, as students can learn these skills on their own.

questions.question: A project manager sends an important project update via email to her team. However, due to a critical server failure at the company, the email is not delivered for several hours, causing confusion and delays. In the communication process model, what does this server failure best represent?

options.b: An encoding error by the sender
options.d: A decoding failure by the receiver

questions.question: During a presentation, a speaker says, "I am completely open to your questions." However, as he says this, he crosses his arms tightly across his chest, avoids making eye contact with the audience, and slightly frowns. How would an astute observer most likely interpret this situation?

options.a: The speaker's verbal message is the most reliable indicator of his true feelings.
options.b: The non-verbal cues (kinesics) are contradicting the verbal message, suggesting defensiveness or unwillingness.
options.c: This is an example of effective use of paralanguage to emphasize a point.
options.d: The communication is successful because the words were clearly spoken.

questions.question: A senior engineer, who is highly respected for his technical skills, proposes a new workflow process to his manager. The manager immediately thinks, "He's an engineer, not a management expert. His ideas on processes are probably not practical," and dismisses the proposal without a fair review. Which specific type of communication barrier is the manager exhibiting?

A teacher wants to explain a complex scientific concept to her students. She decides to use a simple analogy, draws a diagram on the whiteboard, and carefully chooses words that the students will understand. This process of converting her thoughts and the complex information into a specific format (words, diagrams, analogy) for the students is known as:

In an international business meeting, a delegate from North America feels uncomfortable because his counterpart from South America is standing very close to him while talking, well within what he considers his 'personal space'. This discomfort and potential miscommunication arise from differing cultural norms related to:
In a formal business letter, what does the abbreviation 'Encl.' signify, and where is it typically placed?

options.a: It stands for 'Enclosed' and is placed below the signature line to indicate that additional documents are included.
options.b: It stands for 'End of Clause' and is placed at the end of the main body to signify the conclusion of the message.
options.c: It stands for 'Enclosed' and is placed just below the recipient's address to inform them immediately about attachments.
options.d: It stands for 'Entry Cleared' and is part of the letterhead, used for internal office tracking purposes.

questions.question: M/s Gupta Electronics purchased 50 mixer grinders from a supplier. Upon delivery, they found 10 units were damaged. If M/s Gupta Electronics writes a letter detailing this issue and requesting replacements, what type of letter would the supplier send in response to resolve the issue favorably?

While composing a professional email, you need to send a copy to your manager for their information, but you do not want the primary recipient to know that your manager has also received it. Which field should you use for your manager's email address?

A college library wants to buy a new set of encyclopedias. They write a letter to a publisher asking for a detailed catalogue, the latest price list, and information about possible discounts for bulk purchases. What specific type of business letter is this?

options.a: An Order Letter, as they have the intention to buy.
options.b: An Inquiry Letter, as they are gathering information before making a purchase decision.
options.c: An Adjustment Letter, as they are asking for a price adjustment (discount).
options.d: A Reply Letter, as they are replying to an advertisement from the publisher.

questions.question: In the closing part of a business letter, which of the following combinations of salutation and complimentary close is considered formally correct in British English?

options.b: Salutation: 'Dear Mr. Sharma', Complimentary Close: 'Yours faithfully'

questions.question: Read the sentence and choose the option that correctly identifies the parts of speech for the underlined words: 'Alas! The incredibly talented artist quickly finished the portrait.'
Select the correct verb form to complete the sentence: 'Neither the team members nor the captain ______ satisfied with the final score of the match.'
Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank, paying close attention to the tense required: 'By the time my parents reached the theatre, the movie ______.'
Identify the sentence where the pronoun is used incorrectly.

options.a: The secret is just between you and me.
options.b: She is the one whom I believe will get the promotion.
options.c: My brother and I are going to the village fair.
options.d: To whom should I address this formal complaint?

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct combination of a preposition and a connector: '______ his lack of experience, he completed the project successfully ______ he received a lot of guidance.'

units.name: Correction of Incorrect Words and Sentences

questions.question: Choose the correctly spelled word which means a place to live, work, or stay in.
Identify the sentence that is grammatically correct.

options.a: Each of the students are responsible for their own work.
options.b: Each of the student is responsible for their own work.
options.c: Each of the students is responsible for his or her own work.
options.d: Each of the students were responsible for their own work.

questions.question: Select the correct option to complete the sentence: The manager's decision will ______ the entire team.
Which of the following sentences is structured correctly and conveys a clear meaning?

options.a: Having finished the assignment, the TV was turned on by me.
options.b: After finishing the assignment, I turned on the TV.
options.c: The TV was turned on, I having finished the assignment.
options.d: I turned on the TV, when finished the assignment.

questions.question: Which of the following words, meaning 'definitely' or 'certainly', is spelled correctly?
